seoTitle: How to Group Outlook Emails by Conversation (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to turn on Outlook's conversation view to bundle related email replies into one thread, so you stop losing track of client follow-ups.
intro: If your inbox is full of disconnected replies from the same conversation, Outlook's conversation view can stack them into a single expandable thread. This guide walks through turning it on, applying it to specific folders, and adjusting how the newest messages are sorted so nothing important gets buried.

steps.heading: Turn on conversation view in the desktop app
steps.body: Open Outlook and enable the conversation grouping option from your view settings so replies on the same subject line start stacking together automatically.

steps.heading: Apply the setting to specific folders
steps.body: Decide whether you want grouping applied only to your inbox or across every folder, then apply the rule accordingly so your other folders aren't affected unexpectedly.

steps.heading: Set up threaded layouts in the web version
steps.body: If you also use Outlook on the web, turn on the equivalent conversation setting there so your threads look the same across desktop and browser.

steps.heading: Adjust reading pane sort order
steps.body: Configure whether the newest message in a thread appears on top or at the bottom, matching how you naturally read replies.

steps.heading: Expand threads and verify your inbox
steps.body: Click the small arrow next to a stacked conversation to expand every message in it, and confirm your inbox now shows organized, grouped rows instead of scattered single emails.

faq.question: Will grouping delete or merge my emails?
faq.answer: No, conversation view only changes how messages are displayed. Every email stays intact and separate; they're just visually bundled together.

faq.question: Can I turn conversation view off again?
faq.answer: Yes, you can disable the grouping setting at any time to go back to a standard chronological list of individual messages.

faq.question: Does conversation view work the same on desktop and web?
faq.answer: Both versions support conversation grouping, but you need to toggle the setting separately in each since they don't always sync automatically.

faq.question: Can I apply conversation view to just one folder?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose to apply the grouping rule to a single folder instead of your entire mailbox.
